title: Coolidge Corner Theatre workers vote to authorize strike as contract negotiations stall
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Coolidge Corner Theatre workers in Brookline voted to authorize a strike amid stalled contract negotiations with management.
Workers seek job protections, including for part-time staff, and negotiations on implementing self-service kiosks.
More than 80% of the union voted for strike authorization, but no strike is imminent as negotiations continue.

title: Alex Cora believes this Red Sox player will slug 30 home runs this season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Alex Cora predicts Red Sox outfielder Wilyer Abreu will hit 30 home runs in the 2026 season.
FanGraphs projects no Red Sox players to reach 20 home runs in 2026; Abreu is projected at 18.
Abreu hit 22 home runs in 115 games last season and excelled in the 2026 World Baseball Classic for Venezuela.

title: New NFL mock draft has Patriots taking ‘fascinating’ offensive tackle with pick No. 31
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots projected to select Arizona State tackle Max Iheanachor with 31st pick in 2026 NFL mock draft.
Iheanachor, 6'6 and 321 pounds, was a full-time starter at tackle and earned Second Team All-Big 12 honors.
Iheanachor's potential as a future NFL starter noted despite limited football experience and recent development.

title: Worcester school officials address racist comments after girls basketball game
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Worcester officials condemned racist comments following a March 6 girls basketball game between South High and Andover High.
Social media posts with racial slurs targeted South High players, prompting a united stand against racism from Worcester leaders.
The NAACP and YWCA criticized the comments, highlighting their damaging effects and calling for protective measures for youth.

title: Rehoboth man allegedly sexually exploited minors, had ‘hundreds’ of illegal images, videos
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Christopher Mota, 29, from Rehoboth, was charged with four counts of sexual exploitation of minors aged 12 to 16.
Authorities found child sexual abuse material on Mota's phone during a border search at Logan International Airport in June 2025.
Mota allegedly admitted to exchanging explicit content with minors and paying them through Apple Cash and Cash App.

title: Jackson Square intersection reopens after burst water main flooded streets
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jackson Square intersection in Jamaica Plain reopened after a water main break caused flooding and road closures.
A 12-inch diameter pipe caused the flooding; repairs took 48 hours to complete, reopening all lanes by Thursday.
Nearby businesses, including three daycares, were evacuated and closed until the flooding was controlled.

title: Former Brigham and Women’s doctor facing new rape charges
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Derrick Todd, 52, was indicted on new charges of three counts of rape and 17 counts of indecent assault involving 11 patients.
Todd, a former Brigham and Women's doctor, allegedly assaulted women aged 20-60 from 2021 to 2023 in Framingham clinics.
Todd faces ongoing legal proceedings, including a class action with over 200 plaintiffs and prior rape charges from 2025.

title: Getting a vehicle ready for Van Life
author: John Paul
bullets: Consider a second battery, preferably lithium iron phosphate, for powering appliances in a converted RAM van.
Use a pure sine wave converter for converting 12-volts to 120-volts when using the auxiliary battery.
Solar panels with a voltage regulator can be added to maintain and charge the auxiliary battery.

title: Charges dropped against former Brockton choral director after alleged victim declines to testify
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Charges against former Brockton choral director Matthew Cunningham were dropped after the alleged victim declined to testify.
Cunningham was arrested in August 2025 for allegedly paying an underage boy for sex, following a joint police investigation.
Brockton Public Schools placed Cunningham on leave after his arrest; he is no longer employed there.

title: 18-year-old and 14-year-old charged in daytime armed robbery in Charlestown
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Davonte Jordan, 18, and a 14-year-old boy were charged with armed robbery in Charlestown involving an elderly woman's purse.
Police arrested the suspects after they fled from officers at Monument St.; the 14-year-old possessed the victim’s credit card.
Jordan pleaded not guilty in court and was held without bail pending a dangerousness hearing, according to court records.
